name: Nonstop: Aaron J, Clarisa Kimskii, DVS1, Juana b2b Elle Dee, Sleep D (live)
description: With summer heat in full effect, Nonstop brings together seven masters of hypnotic techno for an extended marathon of hazy dancefloor delirium. Aaron J guides us through the threshold, with whispering arboreal textures forming a psychedelic canopy above the steadily pulsing world-rhythm. Taking control at peak time is Australian duo Sleep D, whose trippy, progressive floor-fillers have become a staple of the festival circuit. The late night hours are ruled by two east coast techno staples, Juana and Elle Dee, locking down the floor with their shared love of deep mesmerizing grooves. NYCέΑβs own Clarisa Kimskii carries into the early morning, triggering second winds with the precision of a skilled techno hypnotist. Finally, Midwest legend DVS1 keeps it locked with a marathon 6-hour set of pulsing percussion and expertly smoothed-out deep sea basslines. /// RSVP is $10 before 11pm and $15 before midnight. An RSVP is good for one person. If you're bringing friends, please have them RSVP here as well. We stop taking RSVPs at 6pm on the day of the party. In order to receive the RSVP discount, you must be at the ticket desk by the cutoff time and not just in line. It's wise to arrive early to make sure you're not standing in line when the time comes. An RSVP does not guarantee entry. Sometimes parties do sell out, so if you want to be sure you'll get in, it's best to grab a ticket in advance. /// Entry is at the discretion of our door staff. Online purchases are limited to four tickets maximum per person. If tickets are sold out here, we always hold a good amount at the door. We will do our best to admit those without tickets but entrance is not guaranteed. Nonstop tickets are divided by entry time. You must arrive during the hours listed on your ticket. For example, if you buy a "10PMέΑΥ4AM" ticket, you must get here between 10pm and 4am. After you've arrived during your designated time window, you can stay as long as you'd like, so long as you abide by our safer space policy (more on that below). You're also welcome to come and go as you please. Re-entry is based on capacity and is not guaranteed. Nonstop attendees can attend Mister Sunday in the backyard starting at 3 pm without buying a separate ticket, and Nonstop re-entry is honored during Mister Sunday. Violence, non-consensual touching, racist, homophobic, transphobic, sexist, ageist or other discriminatory language, and leering are not allowed within our walls or in our back yard. If someone says or does something to make you feel uncomfortable while youέΑβre here, let us know. /// Accessibility: The wheelchair entrance to our indoor space is in the driveway, which is the access point most commonly used to enter Nowadays. During parties the driveway gate is locked, so please ask staff at the street entrance for assistance with entry. All of our bathrooms are gender-neutral. There is one accessible stall indoors. Just ask any Nowadays staff member for the key, and they can show you the way. During parties, seating is available both on and off the dance floor. While we donέΑβt have a dedicated quiet space indoors during parties, the booths next to coat check are a little quieter, and the deck and back yard a little quieter than that. Most nighttime parties include the use of strobe lights and water based haze.

name: UFO95 live / Altinbas / cotton / Shaun J. Wright / Colored Craig / Nat Wendell
description: UFO95 returns to the room for a live set. The French artist described his recent έΑεDissolutionέΑζ EP, released on his own TSSRCT label as inhabiting έΑεa territory where noise meets precision.έΑζ We couldnέΑβt have said it better ourselves. Across its four tracks, UFO95 displays his mastery as a sonic sculptor. All his work is constructed with a rigor and clarity of purpose that is matched only by his flair for raw, emotionally-charged gestures. His live sets have appeared at nearly every techno venue of consequence, and in addition to his self-released material, his work has found a home on such legendary imprints as Tresor, Mord and WSNWG. Altinbas makes his debut. The Belgian artist is a resident at the Brussels hub Fuse, where he has honed his elegant, spacious sound. Across a string of releases on Ostgut Ton, Token and Non Series, Altinbas crafts pure, linear techno that hits with supple power. Cotton returns to freak the system. Of the generation of DJs who seized control over the last half decade, Cotton has emerged as one of the most interesting and unique. A true powerhouse, Cotton can balance bracing impact with the exploratory and uncanny in surprising ways. Shaun J. Wright drops in to deliver an unimpeachable masterclass. Wright embodies house musicέΑβs soaring, bombastic and galvanizing spirit. At its core, house is a music that demands liberation through movement, and Wright is a direct inheritor to that lineage. Raised in Chicago, WrightέΑβs music and DJ hit with an urgency that is paradoxically timeless - the urgency of the human soulέΑβs vital need for unrestrained expression. Colored Craig is a fellow traveler and a kindred spirit to Wright. Having recently moved from LA to NYC, Craig has upped the ante of soulful, sensuous house music for the rest of us. A choreographer as well as a DJ, Craig grasps the essence of movement and corporeal sensation in a way few can. In his hands, tracks come alive, blooming with a freshness that feels revelatory. Nat Wendell seals the deal. Returning to the room from his home in Berlin, the London-born artist tweaks the formula with a perfect, tough-and-tender flair. His hometown is known for its particularly affecting sense of defiance, a posture that winds through everything from punk, acid house, drum n bass and grime. Wendell captures this ineffable spirit in his own way, YouέΑβll know it when you hear it. έΨι No photos or videos έΨι Ticket purchase does not guarantee entrance έΨι No phone use on the dancefloor έΨι The dance floor is for dancing, not conversation έΨι No glowing/flashing clothes or items έΨι Respect other people's space, no shuffling έΨι Consent is mandatory έΨι Zero tolerance for racism, homophobia, transphobia, sexism, ableism or any form of discrimination έΨι Take care of each other, alert our staff if somebody isn't feeling well έΨι Violating the house rules will result in removal PLEASE REVIEW HOUSE POLICIES AT LINK BELOW

name: Lee Foss: Repopulate Mars 10 Years w Deeper Purpose, Wheats & More
description: Gray Area welcomes Lee Foss back to New York for a special celebration marking 10 years of Repopulate Mars at 99 Scott. Born and raised in Chicago, Foss emerged from the city that gave birth to house music before becoming one of the genre's most influential label founders and tastemakers. After meeting Jamie Jones in Ibiza during the early 2000s, the pair would go on to launch Hot Natured and Hot Creations, helping define a generation of house music throughout the following decade. In 2016, Foss launched Repopulate Mars. What began as a label soon evolved into a larger creative platform, introducing audiences to artists such as John Summit, Mau P, Michael Bibi, Joshwa, and many others. Alongside its musical output, Repopulate Mars became recognizable for its futuristic visual language inspired by life on another planet, combining chrome aesthetics, science fiction imagery, and immersive event production. Joining Foss for the anniversary celebration is Deeper Purpose, whose connection to the label makes his appearance particularly fitting. The London producer has released multiple records through the Repopulate Mars ecosystem while building an international following through releases on Catch & Release, Toolroom, Experts Only, and Insomniac. His performances have taken him to venues and festivals including Hi Ibiza, Club Space, EDC Las Vegas, Day Trip, and Pacha New York. Also appearing is Wheats, one of the UK's most talked about emerging producers. Through releases on BOX RED, Hot Creations, and Solid Grooves, he has earned support from artists including Jamie Jones, Michael Bibi, and Marco Carola. Philadelphia's Oscar N joins the lineup after chart success on Beatport and performances at Amsterdam Dance Event and Elements Music Festival. Opening duties will be handled by local favorites Nadia and TheConnect. Taking over the industrial setting of 99 Scott, this event brings together artists connected to the past, present, and future of Repopulate Mars for a rare celebration of one of house music's most influential labels.

name: The Presence of Attention with John Hwang
description: The Presence of Attention is a session in breath (guided by Sylvie Koo), story, and deep listening έΑΦ played entirely on vinyl. We begin with a short breathwork session to settle the body and tune the ear inward. From there, across three movements έΑΦ breath, touch, and space έΑΦ we learn to hear what usually goes unnoticed: the inhale before a voice, the decay of a struck note, the space around a sound. Each record is chosen for what it reveals when you truly pay attention, and for the story behind it. Artist Bio: John Hwang is a music curator and a content creator with a channel devoted to the stories behind records. He builds listening experiences that treat attention as the medium έΑΦ pairing deep cuts with the histories that make them matter. His sessions draw on a personal vinyl collection. Sylvie Koo is a breathwork facilitator and harmonium player, trained at ALCHEMY Yoga & Meditation Center in Bali, who guides sessions that pair conscious breathing with sound.

name: Carozilla, Jinmoo, BIGTER (Kompakt Records), James Park - Dead Letter No.9
description: Saturday, July 25th Dead Letter No. 9 welcomes a special gathering of artists connecting Seoul and NYC for an afternoon of soulful selectors, deep grooves and vinyl mastery. From 5PMέΑΥ9PM, the Foyer hosts Carozilla, Jinmoo, Bigter (Kompakt Records), and James Park for a golden hour session celebrating Korea's thriving underground dance music scene alongside some of New York's finest record lovers. Making the trip from Seoul are Jinmoo, founder of the iconic Kompakt Record Bar and longtime member of the influential 360 Sounds collective, and Bigter, whose releases on Kompakt Records have earned him recognition for his hypnotic blend of melodic house and understated techno. Joining them are Carozilla, the Miami-born, New York-based DJ whose vibrant sets weave together global disco, acid jazz, soulful house, and electronic grooves, and James Park, a longtime New York selector and record collector known for his warm, vinyl-inspired approach to house, Balearic, and disco. The gates are up, the front patio is open, and summer is in full swing. Grab a frozen cocktail, settle in for Happy Hour from 5PMέΑΥ7PM, and let the music carry you from late afternoon into golden hour. Whether you're stopping by for a drink or staying for the full session, there's no better spot to kick off your Saturday. Music 5PM-9PM | Free w/ RSVP | 21+ Kitchen open late

name: Spinall Open-Air w. Vonnie Mack, WEMI, & More
description: Gray Area welcomes SPINALL to H0L0 Yard on July 25 for an open air session that brings together Afrobeats, Afro house, amapiano, and club music from across the African diaspora. Over the last decade, SPINALL has become one of the central figures behind Afrobeats' rise beyond Nigeria. As both a DJ and producer, he's built a catalog that stretches across the genre's biggest names, collaborating with Wizkid, Burna Boy, Davido, Tiwa Savage, Fireboy DML, Sean Paul, Omah Lay, Tyla, Summer Walker, DJ Snake, and many more. His productions have become fixtures in clubs around the world, while his performances have taken him to Coachella, Glastonbury, Lollapalooza, ACL Festival, SXSW, and major festivals throughout Africa, Europe, and North America. Along the way, he became the first Afrobeats DJ to perform across both weekends of Coachella, adding another milestone to a career recognized with multiple Afrimma, Soundcity MVP, and Beatz Awards. The supporting lineup reflects the depth of New York's African dance music community. Brooklyn's Vonnie Mack brings a wide ranging selection shaped by amapiano, Afro house, UK jungle, and global club sounds. As the founder of The Yes Honey Collective and MACK SAUCE, she's helped build spaces that celebrate music, community, and representation while appearing on H├ΨR, Rinse FM, Foundation FM, and Refuge Worldwide. Also joining the bill is WEMI, the New York born DJ and producer with Nigerian roots whose Afro house and Afro tech selections have made her a familiar name across the city's dance floors. Sydni Luv, the first Nigerian female DJ based in New York City, continues to champion Afrobeats through her acclaimed NAIRA party series and appearances at AfroNation. Rounding out the lineup is Tia Mobley, completing a bill that highlights both established names and the next generation of selectors. Set inside H0L0 Yard's intimate outdoor setting, this is a chance to experience one of Afrobeats' most influential DJs alongside artists helping shape the sound of New York's Afro diaspora community today.

name: Track Maintenance 10: Tammy Lakkis, Akanbi, Sanjay Nara, Marios
description: Track Maintenance is turning 2!!! To mark the occasion weέΑβve spent the early summer making massive infrastructure improvements to the Third Rail SoundSystem (tm). The project was a success and the system now features two custom built multiple-entry-horns, making the Third Rail Soundsystem (tm) a 100% custom stack. Other infrastructure improvements include upgraded lights and fog capacity to ensure a proper good time. Joining us for the night will be four DJέΑβs that we really love and respect: Coming all the way from the D is ascendant DJ and Producer Tammy Lakkis. As a producer, Tammy is known for her excellent debut EP, Notice, released on Shigeto's Portage Garage Sounds. As a DJ, Tammy hosts several residencies in Detroit, most notably, Monday is the New Monday at Motor City Wine. Tammy excels at weaving together seemingly disparate sounds έΑΥ she can go from floating house to psychedelic percussion to sexy electro with ease. Her Lot Mass set from this past movement is already the stuff of legend... TM is also thrilled to welcome the rave man himself, Akanbi. Akanbi is a personal rave hero of TM because heέΑβs been organizing the seminal Groovy Groovy party with DJ Temporary for a remarkable 10+ years. Much of TMέΑαs inspiration to throw DIY parties came from attending Groovy Groovy and we believe that our values are quite aligned: music, love, and a transformation of space are all necessary ingredients to make the rave impactful. In terms of what you might hear: sensual rollers, insistent percussion, and deep thumping low frequencies all frequent an Akanbi set, but nothing is guaranteed. The one constant with Akanbi is that you must be ready to sweat. Joining us from Uptown is rising DJ, Bassist, and Producer Sanjay Nara. In both his sets and productions, Sanjay is influenced by his desires of liberation and expression, wielding batida and club music inflected with his own blend of bounce, Desi culture, and nostalgia. HeέΑβs self-released a large catalog of remixes and edits. He holds a residency on Particle FM where he hosts Nara Radio. Track Maintenance co-conductor Marios rounds out the lineup and offers an important announcement: Greece is an island. Some tickets will be reserved at the door, but securing an advance ticket is the only way to guarantee entry. This is a DIY event and your ticket helps sustain us. However, as always, no one will be turned away for lack of funds (email Track.Maintenance.NYC@gmail.com to get in touch).

name: PowerHaus presents: Hotter Than July
description: PowerHaus presents Hotter Than July, a free daytime dance party in the park celebrating house, disco, and dance music. Bridge, CRVM, and Wolf Spritzer each bringing their individual worlds together for one unified experience rooted in joy, community, and the dance floor. We cannot wait to dance with you all. Free and open to all. Family friendly. Set times: 2 -2:45pm Wolf Spritzer 2:45 - 3:30 CRVM 3:30 - 4:15 B3B 4:15 - 6pm BRIDGE Get to know our DJs: BRIDGE is a Brooklyn-based DJ and cultural curator delivering high-energy house and disco sets rooted in joy, self-expression, and storytelling. Influenced by classic house, gospel, disco, and ballroom, her vocally driven style honors legends like Barbara Tucker, Martha Wash, and Sylvester while connecting generations on the dance floor. Founder of UPLIFTED and co-founder of PowerHaus, she has brought her sound to Knockdown Center, Public Records, Nowadays, Elsewhere, and beyond. CRVM is a Haitian-American DJ and Brooklyn native weaving deep house, Afro house, and Latin sounds rooted in the African diaspora. His sets move as spiritual journeys where rhythm becomes ritual and movement becomes release. Co-founder of PowerHaus, he has brought his sound to The Lot Radio, Xanadu, Elsewhere, and Jupiter Disco. Wolf Spritzer is a Brooklyn DJ and lifelong bassist fusing classic disco and contemporary house into funk-driven, hard-hitting dance floor sets. Already released on five labels this year, his sound has been shaped by his PowerHaus co-founders and countless nights playing together across the city. As his muse JB would say: "ain't it funky?" name: Victor Simonelli, CHRIS LEWIS, TEE EM DEE, D'Sean - Dead Letter No.9
description: Some DJs play records. Some DJs helped write the history of house music. Born and raised in Brooklyn, Victor Simonelli has spent over four decades shaping New York's underground dance scene. As a producer, remixer, label owner, and DJ, he's worked alongside legends like Frankie Knuckles, David Morales, Danny Krivit, and Todd Terry, while helping launch and mentor artists who would go on to define generations of dance music. He has released music through legendary imprints including Nu Groove, Fourth Floor, Strictly Rhythm, and his own West Side Records. His catalog includes classics like "I Know A Place" (Sound of One), "Feel So Right" (Solution), and Groove Committee's "I Want You To Know," records that helped define the sound of New York. His production and remix credits include work with artists such as David Bowie, Michael Jackson, Quincy Jones, Chaka Khan, Ray Charles, Deborah Harry, Talking Heads, James Brown, Madonna and many more. From 12AMέΑΥ3AM in Cargo, Victor takes over for a rare extended set. Equal parts history lesson, dance floor workout, and masterclass in house music, this is a chance to experience one of the true pioneers of New York's underground scene in an intimate room built for deep listening and serious movement. From 10PM-12AM Chris Lewis opens CARGO. Known for his deep, soulful approach to house music and decades spent shaping dance floors across New York and beyond, Chris Lewis opens the night with a masterfully curated journey through house, disco, and underground club sounds. The perfect warm-up before Victor Simonelli takes over from midnight. In the Foyer, two New York selectors guide the foyer from open to close. TEE EM DEE (Third Floor Sounds) opens with deep, soulful house and impeccable selections. From 12AMέΑΥ3AM, D'Sean takes the reins, bringing a sound shaped by years of digging and collecting, blending soulful house, Detroit techno, disco edits, and driving rhythms into a set that is equal parts dynamic, uplifting, and built for the dance floor. Venue opens at 5PM | Happy Hour 5PM-8PM | Music at 9PM Kitchen open late | 21+
